title: Sweet Seymour Skinners Baadasssss Song

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's signature opening sequence mishap
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation and scream
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase and physical comedy - not original within the show but perfectly character-integrated and economical

jokes.text: Mom, you're always trying to give me potatoes. What is it with you? - I just think they're neat.
jokes.setup: Bart questions Marge's obsession with offering him potatoes
jokes.punchline: Marge's simple, innocent explanation that she just thinks they're neat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Marge's endearing simplicity contrasted with Bart's exasperation. The deadpan delivery of 'neat' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Bart, that's a bran muffin!
jokes.setup: Homer has been identifying geological specimens for Bart
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Homer has mistaken a bran muffin for a rock specimen
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from actual rocks to food - shows Homer's obliviousness in a visual way

jokes.text: Greetings, fellow geode-ologist.
jokes.setup: Martin seeing Bart with what he assumes are geological specimens
jokes.punchline: Martin's nerdy portmanteau combining geode and geologist
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Martin's character - nerdy wordplay delivered with genuine enthusiasm

jokes.text: What has four legs and ticks? - A walking clock? - A walking clock! I'd wager he has some variety of walking clock in that box.
jokes.setup: Bart poses a riddle expecting them to guess 'dog'
jokes.punchline: The students' literal interpretation leading to 'walking clock'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - the students' literal thinking subverts Bart's expectation

jokes.text: My raisin roundies!
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper is given Martin's homemade cookies
jokes.punchline: Martin's dismayed reaction at his precious baked goods being fed to a dog
jokes.explanation: Martin's earnest distress over his homemade treats - character-appropriate but fairly standard

jokes.text: You're doing it wrong! You gotta pet him hard so he can feel it.
jokes.setup: Students are gently petting the dog
jokes.punchline: Bart's misguided advice on how to pet dogs
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's lack of proper pet care knowledge - mild character comedy

jokes.text: Ha! It thinks it's people.
jokes.setup: The dog sneezes
jokes.punchline: Nelson's amused observation treating the sneeze as human-like behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line - economical and captures his amused detachment. The phrase became quotable.

jokes.text: More testicles mean more iron.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper is sniffing around the cafeteria
jokes.punchline: Doris's matter-of-fact comment about questionable meat while the dog investigates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive humor - darkly funny commentary on school lunch quality delivered with perfect deadpan casualness. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: He was going to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Miss Hoover reminds Ralph about falsely reporting Snagglepuss outside
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent clarification that Snagglepuss was using the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph moment - his innocent misunderstanding of cartoon characters as real

jokes.text: I mean, it sounds so made-up. 'Yom Kip Pur.'
jokes.setup: Skinner on phone explaining why he doubted a student's excuse
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation and dismissal of Yom Kippur as fake-sounding
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of cultural ignorance - Skinner's mispronunciation and dismissal of a major Jewish holiday is both cringe-worthy and revealing

jokes.text: What? Have you gone waxy in your 'beester'? I cannot fit in the wee vent, ya croquet-playin' mint muncher!
jokes.setup: Skinner orders Willie to go into the vent
jokes.punchline: Willie's colorful Scottish insults and logical objection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie dialogue - creative Scottish-flavored insults combined with practical logic. 'Croquet-playin' mint muncher' is memorably specific.

jokes.text: Grease yourself up and go in, you-- you guff-speaking work slacker. - Ooh, good comeback.
jokes.setup: Skinner attempts to insult Willie back
jokes.punchline: Chalmers sarcastically praising Skinner's weak comeback attempt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's inability to match Willie's colorful language, with Chalmers' dry commentary

jokes.text: Then grease me up, woman! - Okeydokey.
jokes.setup: Willie agrees to be greased to fit in the vent
jokes.punchline: Doris's casual acceptance of this bizarre request
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor - Willie's dramatic demand contrasted with Doris's matter-of-fact compliance creates perfect comedy

jokes.text: There's nary an animal alive that can outrun a greased Scotsman.
jokes.setup: Willie emerges from the vents after chasing the dog
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration about greased Scottish speed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Willie line - combines his Scottish pride with absurdist logic. The specific phrasing is both funny and memorable, perfectly character-integrated.

jokes.text: 'Tis more dizzyin' than the belfry at St. Corgegluynwaagen's.
jokes.setup: Willie is disoriented from the vent chase
jokes.punchline: His reference to an impossibly-named Scottish church
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Willie's elaborate fake Scottish place name adds authenticity to his background while being absurd

jokes.text: The low test scores, class after class of ugly, ugly children!
jokes.setup: Chalmers lists his complaints about the school
jokes.punchline: His shockingly inappropriate criticism of the children's appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversive humor - completely inappropriate complaint that escalates beyond professional concerns to personal appearance, perfectly shocking

jokes.text: Why, looking into this loveable mutt's eyes just melts my heart. Seymour, all is forgiven.
jokes.setup: Chalmers was furious and about to fire Skinner
jokes.punchline: Complete personality reversal upon seeing the cute dog
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how easily swayed authority figures can be by cute animals

jokes.text: Make way for Willie! I said make way for Willie, ya bloated gasbag! - Seymour. - Hmm? - You're fired!
jokes.setup: Willie bursts in calling someone a bloated gasbag, presumably Skinner
jokes.punchline: Chalmers takes it personally and fires Skinner instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - Willie's insult meant for others accidentally triggers Skinner's firing. Good escalation and misdirection.

jokes.text: I said make way for Willie, ya bloated gasbag!
jokes.setup: Willie trying to get through with his announcement
jokes.punchline: Willie's insulting follow-up when ignored
jokes.explanation: Good Willie insult but not as creative as his earlier material

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Did-- Did you just call me a liar? - No. I said you were fired. - Oh. That's much worse.
jokes.setup: Skinner mishears 'fired' as 'liar'
jokes.punchline: His realization that being fired is actually worse than being called a liar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's priorities and his delayed realization of the severity

jokes.text: I thought I'd be jumping for joy the day Skinner left... but now all I have is this weird, hot feeling in the back of my head. - That's guilt.
jokes.setup: Bart describes an unfamiliar physical sensation
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact identification of guilt, which Bart has never experienced
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - Bart's unfamiliarity with guilt is both funny and believable. Lisa's clinical diagnosis adds to the humor.

jokes.text: All right, you little punks. Prick up your freakin' ears... because I'm only gonna be saying this once.
jokes.setup: Leopold appears to be the tough new principal
jokes.punchline: His aggressive, inappropriate language to elementary school children
jokes.explanation: Good contrast character - inappropriately aggressive tone for a school setting, sets up the misdirection

jokes.text: with your new principal, Ned Flanders.
jokes.setup: Leopold's tough-guy introduction builds expectation he'll be principal
jokes.punchline: Reveals Ned Flanders as the actual new principal
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Leopold's aggressive setup makes Ned's appointment completely unexpected and funny

jokes.text: Well, tippity-top o' the a.m. to every-good-body here. As chairman of the P.T.A., I am de-didilly-lighted to take over... and I think I can put the 'pal' back in principal.
jokes.setup: Ned's overly cheerful introduction as new principal
jokes.punchline: His corny wordplay about putting 'pal' back in 'principal'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders dialogue - overly cheerful with corny wordplay. Character-appropriate but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Yeah, and I'll put the 'super' back in superintendent. - [Someone Coughs] - It's the same exact joke. What gives, Leo?
jokes.setup: Leopold tries to copy Ned's wordplay format
jokes.punchline: A student calls out the lazy joke repetition directly
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - the show calls out its own lazy joke construction through a student, breaking the fourth wall cleverly

jokes.text: Well, let me just say I want to put the 'stu' back in students.
jokes.setup: Continuing the wordplay pattern established earlier
jokes.punchline: Ned's unintentionally inappropriate truncation making 'stu' instead of something meaningful
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag but with diminishing returns - the wordplay doesn't work as well

jokes.text: I call it Billy and the Clone-osaurus.
jokes.setup: Skinner describes his novel about a futuristic dinosaur amusement park
jokes.punchline: His terrible title that's obviously derivative of Jurassic Park
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of derivative creative work - the terrible title perfectly captures failed attempts to copy popular media

jokes.text: Oh, you have got to be kidding, sir. First you think of an idea that has already been done. Then you give it a title that nobody could possibly like. Didn't you think this through? ...on the best-seller list for 18 months! Every magazine cover had it! ...most popular movies of all time, sir! What were you thinking? I mean, thank you. Come again.
jokes.setup: Apu reacts to Skinner's book idea
jokes.punchline: His detailed breakdown of why the idea is terrible, ending with his standard customer service sign-off
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Apu's passionate critique of the obvious Jurassic Park ripoff, followed by his polite customer service recovery

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. Were you drinking Slice or Yoo-hoo?
jokes.setup: Ned accidentally spills something while disciplining Bart
jokes.punchline: His overly polite concern about what specific drink he spilled
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy - Ned's politeness even during disciplinary moments, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Well, these new Italian loafers will make short work of it.
jokes.setup: Skinner confidently approaches what he thinks is a flaming bag
jokes.punchline: His pride in his shoes before stepping in dog waste
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for physical comedy - Skinner's confidence before the obvious pratfall

jokes.text: We got Skinner's underpants! And there's nothing you can do about it, Mr. Ex-principal. - That's not true. I can buy a new pair. - No, I can't. I needed those. I really did.
jokes.setup: Bullies taunting Skinner with stolen underwear
jokes.punchline: Skinner's failed attempt at dignity followed by pathetic admission of poverty
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Skinner's attempt to maintain dignity crumbles immediately, revealing his desperate situation

jokes.text: I was trying to get Joey Heatherton to put on some pants, for God's sake!
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining how he got shot during a Bob Hope show in Vietnam
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about Joey Heatherton's lack of pants during a military entertainment show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail - the specific celebrity reference and moral outrage about pants during a combat situation is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Ned Flanders actually eliminated detention... and put the whole school on the honor system? - Yeah. The teachers are afraid to leave the faculty lounge.
jokes.setup: Skinner learns about Ned's progressive policies
jokes.punchline: The immediate consequence of teachers being trapped in their own lounge
jokes.explanation: Good logical escalation - shows the natural consequence of removing all discipline from school

jokes.text: but they might have been saying 'skim milk.' - Yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to comfort Skinner about whether people mention him
jokes.punchline: Skinner's desperate acceptance of even this thin consolation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's desperation for any connection to his former life

jokes.text: Mr. Kookalamanza and-a some real ugly kid.
jokes.setup: Restaurant worker announcing Skinner's arrival
jokes.punchline: His casual insult of Bart as 'some real ugly kid'
jokes.explanation: Standard insult humor - economical but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Not only am I not learnin'... I'm forgettin' stuff I used to know.
jokes.setup: Bart describing the school's deterioration under Ned
jokes.punchline: The idea that lack of education is actually making him dumber
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of educational decline - the idea of actively becoming dumber is both funny and plausible

jokes.text: My water dish is empty!
jokes.setup: The school has become completely lawless
jokes.punchline: Milhouse has apparently regressed to thinking he's a pet
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - shows how far the school has fallen that students have regressed to animal behavior

jokes.text: Uh, they don't have them group toilets here no more, do they?
jokes.setup: New army recruits arriving
jokes.punchline: Recruit's inappropriate question about communal facilities
jokes.explanation: Standard military comedy trope - not particularly original but establishes the low quality of recruits

jokes.text: Oh, man! Ned spilled ink all over my poems! He's a real flat tire. I mean a cube, man. He's putting us on the train to Squaresville, Mona.
jokes.setup: Student complaining about Ned's accident
jokes.punchline: His use of outdated 1950s beatnik slang
jokes.explanation: Good period humor - the anachronistic beatnik language is unexpected and specific

jokes.text: I don't know, but I've been told - The Parthenon is mighty old - How old - We don't know - That's real good but needs improvement
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to teach recruits a proper marching cadence
jokes.punchline: The educational but unheroic content about ancient architecture
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Skinner brings his educational background to military cadences, creating inappropriately academic content

jokes.text: Sherlock Holmes had his Dr. Moriarty. Mountain Dew has its Mello Yello. Even Maggie has that baby with the one eyebrow.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the concept of nemesis to Bart
jokes.punchline: The escalation from literary to commercial to absurdly specific baby rivalry
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and specificity - moves from classic literature to soda brands to baby rivalries, perfectly absurd final example

jokes.text: Sixteen brand-new gas pumps! At last we can compete with the Gas-N-Gulp.
jokes.setup: Apu celebrating his new gas pumps
jokes.punchline: They're immediately destroyed by military ordnance
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic timing - Apu's pride immediately followed by destruction, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Up yours, Sergeant! - Actually, it's exactly as I remembered it.
jokes.setup: Recruit disrespects Skinner
jokes.punchline: Skinner's matter-of-fact acceptance that military insubordination hasn't changed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's resigned acceptance of military reality

jokes.text: Just make a pass at your commanding officer. - Done and done. And I mean done.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests how to get discharged from the army
jokes.punchline: Skinner's immediate acceptance and emphasis on completion
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate desperation from Skinner, though the homosexual panic trope feels dated

jokes.text: A prayer! A prayer in a public school! God has no place within these walls... just like facts have no place within organized religion!
jokes.setup: Chalmers reacts to hearing what sounds like prayer
jokes.punchline: His anti-religious rant with the ironic comparison to facts and religion
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversive humor - Chalmers' passionate anti-religious stance with the ironic counterpoint about facts and organized religion. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You really think it's a good idea to give that freak his job back?
jokes.setup: Leopold questioning Skinner's rehiring
jokes.punchline: The aggressive tough guy calling someone else a freak
jokes.explanation: Mild irony from Leopold - the inappropriate tough guy calling normal Skinner a freak

jokes.text: You really think it's a good idea to give that freak his job back?
jokes.setup: Skinner being reinstated
jokes.punchline: Leopold calling Skinner a freak when Leopold was clearly more unhinged
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the truly unstable character calls Skinner abnormal

tmdb_overview: Bart takes his dog to school, which gets Skinner fired, which in turn leaves Bart missing his archnemesis.
insight: Seymour's redemption arc carries 48 jokes at moderate density, leaning hard on character escalation.
